codeReview.summary: The implementation itself removes the raw-token refresh/reset fallbacks, retires `cid` emission from new JWTs, enforces canonical company-code binding, and deletes the retired superadmin forgot alias from the live contract. The blocking gap is in the regression proof: the new cross-flow test that is supposed to prove `forgot -> reset -> login -> refresh -> /auth/me` still works bypasses the forgot-issued token by inserting its own digest row directly with `JdbcTemplate`, so it does not actually verify that the forgot path still produces a usable digest-backed reset token after the compatibility removal.

issues.description: `canonicalRecoveryFlow_forgotResetLoginRefreshAndMe_succeeds_withDigestBackedResetToken()` calls `postForgot(...)` and then manually inserts a digest row with `jdbcTemplate.update(...)` instead of consuming the reset token produced by the forgot flow. That leaves VAL-CROSS-001 / the feature's required canonical recovery proof unmet: the test would still pass even if `/auth/password/forgot` stopped persisting a usable digest-backed reset token after the compatibility-path removal.

summary: FAIL. The product code looks aligned with canonical-only auth behavior, but the new recovery-flow regression test does not actually prove forgot-issued digest tokens still work because it injects its own token row after the forgot call.
